core_mechanism: degree-biased node selection via random-neighbor (edge-following) sampling that exploits the friendship-paradox effect — following a link from a random node preferentially reaches higher-degree nodes — to approximate optimal targeted removal of hubs using only local information
brief_reason: The abstract's method picks a random node and immunizes a neighbor with more links than itself (or above a degree threshold), a purely local edge-following sampling rule that biases toward high-degree nodes and nearly matches the global highest-degree targeting scheme.
